articles.title: Steve Jobs had a simple question he asked every job candidate and it had nothing to do with work
articles.description: Steve Jobs had a unique hiring approach known as the 'beer test'. He believed personality and the ability to connect were as crucial as skills. Jobs would gauge if he could comfortably have a casual conversation with a candidate. This instinct-driven method helped him find the right people for Apple. Other CEOs also employ personality assessments.

articles.title: Fortnite Overwatch Power Hour (May 16, 2026): Start timings for all regions

Timings vary by region, with specific slots for the Americas, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, offering a unique team-oriented gameplay experience.

articles.title: Quote of the day by humorist Mark Twain: ‘If you pick up a starving dog and make him prosperous he will not bite you. This is...’
articles.description: Mark Twain, born Samuel Clemens, was a renowned American writer and humorist. He captured societal flaws and human nature through his stories and sharp remarks. Twain's works, including Tom Sawyer and Huckleberry Finn, transformed American fiction. He faced personal loss and bankruptcy but repaid debts through lectures. Twain died in 1910, remembered for his wit and moral insight.

articles.title: India-UAE Sign Major Energy Pact As UAE To Store 30 Million Barrels In India’s Strategic Oil Reserve
articles.description: India and the UAE have signed a major strategic energy agreement under which Abu Dhabi will store up to 30 million barrels of crude oil in India’s Strategic Petroleum Reserve. The announcement came after talks between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi. The two countries also discussed gas reserves, LPG supplies, defence cooperation and investments amid rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia.

articles.title: BIG BREAKING: Major Anti-Naxal Operation Success in Chhattisgarh
articles.description: In a major development from Chhattisgarh, security forces achieved a significant success during an anti-Naxal operation in the Kanker district. More than 50 hidden weapons, a huge quantity of ammunition, and several Improvised Explosive Devices (IEDs) were recovered during continuous search operations carried out over the last 15 days. The operation was jointly conducted by the Kanker Police, District Reserve Guard (DRG), Bastar Fighters, and the Border Security Force (BSF). Security personnel carried out raids at multiple suspected Naxal hideouts located in different forest areas of the district. During the searches, the forces seized explosives and arms that had allegedly been hidden by Naxalite groups for future attacks. Officials described the recovery as a major blow to Naxal activities in the region and said that anti-Naxal operations would continue with full intensity to maintain peace and strengthen security in the affected areas.
